title: alchemilla alpestris subsp. montana
intro: Alchemilla alpestris subsp. montana, also known as Alpine Lady's Mantle, is a plant species in the Rosaceae family that thrives in high-altitude mountainous regions. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and fighting bacterial infections, thanks to its high content of flavonoids and tannins. The health benefits of Alchemilla alpestris subsp. montana include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and salves, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal usage.
